_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how authors and illustrators make stories exciting, moving and absorbing — through tension, character development and setting.

_source.w: We are learning how authors and illustrators make stories exciting, moving and absorbing — through tension, character development and setting.
y: Understanding how published authors create engaging stories helps us apply the same techniques.
Analysing craft builds our appreciation of literature and our skills as critical readers.
These insights directly improve our own creative writing.
eg: We identify how an author builds tension through pacing — short sentences during action, longer ones during reflection.
We trace how a character develops throughout a story and what causes them to change.
We discuss how an illustrator's use of colour and perspective creates mood and emotion.
We find the turning point in a story and analyse how the author builds towards it.
We compare how two authors create suspense using different techniques.
We experiment with using plot tension, detailed characters and vivid settings in our own stories.
